IBEW.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Indoor Locals — Tuesday, July 14, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** is running hot on two massive data center builds. Meade Electric's Meta Data Center play in Lebanon hits **$53.80/hr** (10% above scale) on 5-12s Monday-Friday plus Saturday 10s — that's real money for a sustained grind. Ermco's Google gig in Mooresville runs 5-10s with Saturday 8s, straight outside new construction. Book depth sits around 188 hands for 43 calls; you're not sitting long here.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** has Port Washington Data Center locked down across four contractors (Gurtz, Cupertino, MJ, Pieper). The premium is clean: **JW wage plus $10/hr base increase** with all three major contractors naming it explicitly. You're looking 50-60 hour weeks, valid WI license required, drug test mandatory. 203 hands on 38 calls means the board's moving steady but not desperate.

**LU-26 Washington, DC** leans heavy on Sterling data center work — JE Richards and FIS both pulling crews for active builds with planned overtime. Aarow's Nolan Building in Springfield offers a break from data center monotony (new construction, EMT work), and per diem sits at **$125/day** with FIS. 40 calls, 185 hands; solid depth.

**LU-481 Indianapolis** and **LU-481 Indianapolis** both pulling from the same Meta/Google infrastructure sprint — the data center boom is real and sustained. **LU-430 Racine, WI** deserves mention: Microsoft Mount Pleasant paying **$62.51/hr** across Gerdes, Pieper, and Staff Electric, all starting late June into July. Wisconsin license or reciprocal required.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** offers the per diem angle: Parsons Electric's Polaris Forge pull pays **$44.80/hr base plus $100/day non-taxed** (GPS radius 100+ miles) on 6-10s with $100-150 incentive spreads. Book's thin at 93 hands for 23 calls, but the dollar per diem is a workaround for take-home math.

Data centers are absorbing hands faster than traditional construction; 961 calls across 129 locals means work's everywhere, but the premium hours and sustained schedules are stacking in the Midwest and mid-Atlantic data center corridor.

LINEMAN.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Lineman Locals — Tuesday, July 14, 2026**

**LU-71 Columbus, OH** is running hot with 32 calls and ~109 hands on the books. NEW RIVER ELECTRIC is moving **$61.44/hr** journeymen into distribution work across two separate calls—Columbus proper (37 hands, **6-10s**, **$125/day per diem**, **$100 attendance bonus**) and Southern Ohio (10 hands, **4-10s optional Fri/Sat**). CDL A required across the board. This is solid base pay with real incentive meat on the bone for night stays.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** has 23 calls needing ~93 hands, anchored by the Polaris Forge data center near Harwood. Parsons Electric is stacking **24 and 21 Inside JW calls at $44.80/hr**—not top-tier pay, but **100+ mile GPS radius, $100/day non-taxed per diem, plus $100-$150 daily incentives (Sat/Sun bumped)** on **6-10s** makes the effective rate move. Hunt Electric's Grand Forks Air Base work offers **4-10s with 8-hour Friday OT**, same scale. Data center construction is where the volume is right now.

**LU-1393 Indianapolis, IN** shows 21 calls with **$64.66/hr** JW scale (ARC American, Wanatah rebuild, 12.5k work). Newkirk Electric's Muncie T-line job pays **$51.79 + $150/day per diem**—transmission underbuilt double-circuit reconductoring, real lineman work.

**LU-474 Memphis, TN** has the Google Data Center gig (West Memphis, AR)—38 Inside JW at **4-10s + 2-8s, $125/day incentive for full attendance**, **double-time OT**. Arkansas license required.

**LU-291 Boise, ID** anchors Micron ID1—16 JW (day shift), 8 QAQC roles, both at **$45.33 + $170/day per diem x 7**, PLA job, **4-10s**. Real money if you're eligible.

Data center build-outs and transmission rebuilds are pulling hands nationwide; utility scale work is where the steady gigs live.

UA.text:
**Trampin the UA Locals — Tuesday, July 14, 2026**

**LU-290 Portland, OR** is moving serious volume with 22 calls hunting 24 hands. Intel's Ronler Acres campus (Tigard/Hillsboro) is soaking up BIM Detailers and tool-install crews through Apollo and Charter Mechanical—steady work for those who can read a model. PermaCold's commercial refrigeration service calls in Portland Metro add reliable dispatch depth if you're cert'd on the cooling side.

**LU-208 Denver, CO** sits fat with 21 calls and 38 hands in the book. Parker Advent Hospital (Olson Plumbing) is running 4/10's for pipefitters—solid day shift, no graveyard grind. Fort St. Vrain Powerhouse with Kiewit is the marquee play: $65/day per diem, nights 5/9's (5pm–2am), but you'll need to pass the 2" super gate test and you're welding. US Eng. Metalworks' fab shop is burning MIG and TIG shifts if you swing both processes.

**LU-213 St. John, NB** has Irving Oil Refinery locked down across multiple contractors—Stuart Olson, Cam, and NMA shops all feeding the same beast. Day shift 4x10's under GPMA Agreement, short-term work but consistent. Book depth around 34 hands means you're not waiting long.

**LU-192 Cheyenne, WY** is tighter (3 calls, 18 hands) but the money's there: Holly Frontier Sinclair refinery shutdown pulling welders and fitters, DISA cert required. PMI's military work (Ellsworth AFB, Fort Meade VA) throws $100/day per diem for days worked—federal gigs always have that cleanup premium.

**LU-525 Las Vegas, NV** has Hansen Mech running the Strip hotels (Venetian, Palazzo, Hard Rock) with steady plumber calls, but it's 6 total calls chasing 27 hands—glut on the book.

Refinery shutdowns and data-center buildouts are pulling fitters and welders west and north; commercial service work (cooling, hospitality MEP) is filling the gaps where fabrication slows.
